methods.description: Setup of field experiments for pumpkin cultivation to study nectar and pollen production.

steps.description: Seeds of C. pepo L. 'Howden' were sown in trays in individual 200 cm3 cells, filled with 2:1 (v/v) vermiculite:peatmos, supplemented with minerals and maintained in a greenhouse.
steps.searchable_summary: Sow pumpkin seeds in trays with vermiculite and peatmos, supplement with minerals, and maintain in greenhouse.

steps.description: Twenty days after sowing, the plants were transferred to the field, which was previously (3 weeks before) fertilized with 100 kg ha-1 of nitrogen in the form of urea.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer seedlings to field fertilized with nitrogen.

methods.description: Collection and analysis of nectar from male pumpkin flowers to evaluate nectar production, sugar concentration, and nectar recovery.

steps.description: Nectar was collected from male flowers with a 10 µL capillary between 7h and 13h.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect nectar from male flowers using capillary tubes.

steps.description: Nectar production per flower was estimated by measuring the length of the nectar column in the capillary and calculated based on the known volume and length of each capillary.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate nectar production by measuring nectar column length in capillary.

steps.description: Sugar concentration in the nectar produced per flower was measured in the field using a hand refractometer.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure sugar concentration in nectar using refractometer.

steps.description: The replacement, or additional secretion of nectar, after removal by the bees, was evaluated by collecting nectar at different times.
steps.searchable_summary: Evaluate nectar replacement by collecting nectar at different times.

methods.description: Analysis of pollen production in male pumpkin flowers to estimate the total number of pollen grains per flower.

steps.description: Male flowers were randomly selected and bagged before anthesis to prevent visits by bees and other insects.
steps.searchable_summary: Select and bag male flowers before anthesis.

steps.description: The anthers of each flower were removed, placed in vials with 70% ethanol and stored in a refrigerator.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove anthers and store in ethanol.

steps.description: The anthers were washed with 70% ethanol until all pollen grains were removed.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash anthers with ethanol to remove pollen grains.

steps.description: Five samples of 50 µL were taken from the pollen suspension and all pollen grains were counted under 60X magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains in samples under magnification.

methods.description: Counting the number of pistillate and staminate flowers produced over the flowering period.

steps.description: Twelve plants were randomly selected to count the number of flowers.
Pistillate and staminate flowers were counted every day from the beginning to the end of flowering.
steps.searchable_summary: Count pistillate and staminate flowers daily.
